title: The Library of Babel
author: Jorge Luis Borges
blurb: An infinite library suggests a universe of texts where meaning is elusive and pattern is a mirage.

title: Perception and the Frame
author: L. Chen
blurb: An essay on how framing devices shape what we call real.

title: The Ones Who Walk Away from Omelas
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
blurb: A parable about joy that rests on a secret cost.

title: Ethics of Complicity
author: M. Navarro
blurb: A short primer on how systems enlist us in their bargains.

title: Footnotes to a Dream
author: A. K. Rowan
blurb: A story that unfolds in footnotes which overtake the main text.

title: Cognitive Dissonance in Narrative Worlds
author: S. Patel
blurb: On how readers hold conflicting truths inside a single world.

title: The City and Its Double
author: M. C. Graves
blurb: A metropolis meets its mirror and cannot tell which side breathes.

title: Walking in the Real-Unreal
author: H. Ito
blurb: Field notes on drifting through thresholds in the everyday city.
